enrichment.summary: Kyle responds to a viewer request about witchcraft, Satanism, and the occult, tracing witchcraft from pre-Christian tribal/pagan religions through Christianity's rise, and arguing each term means something distinct. He believes tribal medicine men had real extrasensory powers, views the occult as hidden-but-positive esoteric knowledge suppressed out of fear, and sees Satanism as mostly a reactive myth-driven rebellion against Christianity. He envisions modern witchcraft evolving into nature-based spiritual practice and extra-sensory development.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: rooted in pre-Christian tribal religion; not evil, will evolve into preserved spiritual traditions
believes they are real — remote viewing, plant-finding visions, out-of-body travel
superior because the golden rule created a scalable civilizational law; should layer onto other traditions, not replace them
simply hidden positive knowledge (e.g. Kabbalah) kept secret to avoid persecution by the ignorant
essentially a simple-minded rebellion against Christianity built on a myth that snowballed, like Slender Man
beneficial technologies compatible with Christianity; unfairly lumped in with harmful practices
stupid and ineffective; just angry people's wishful thinking
salvia and mushrooms in natural settings are a legitimate form of modern witchcraft / spirit contact
ancient gods were likely aliens who gave laws to the Jews and sent Jesus
